title: Add --resume flag for interrupted agent sessions
source: Claude Code session continuity and conversation resume patterns (Anthropic CLI docs, 2025-2026)
description: When a Morty agent times out or fails mid-execution, all progress is lost and the ticket must be re-delegated from scratch. Claude Code supports `--resume` and `--continue` flags to resume conversations. By persisting the Claude session ID for each delegation and supporting resumption, interrupted work can be continued rather than restarted — saving tokens, time, and avoiding duplicate file changes.
proposed_changes: In delegate.py, modify `delegate_to_agent()` (line 778) to: (1) Capture the Claude session ID from the subprocess output/metadata and store it in the ticket JSON under `session_id`; (2) Add a `--resume` flag to the delegate CLI argument parser; (3) When `--resume` is passed for a ticket that has a stored `session_id`, use `claude --resume <session_id>` instead of `claude -p` with the full prompt, appending only a continuation instruction like 'Continue working on this ticket. Pick up where you left off.'; (4) In the error handler (line 928), when a timeout occurs, save the session_id to the ticket and set status to 'interrupted' instead of 'failed', allowing the user to resume with `python delegate.py --resume TICKET-ID`.
